questions.question:
Find the odd one out in the set: {19, 37, 21, 17, 25, 29, 31}

A. 21
B. 37
C. 29
D. 23

understand.plain: We are given a set of numbers and must identify the one that does not share the common property of the rest. Most of the numbers in the set are prime numbers (divisible only by 1 and themselves). The number 21 is the only composite number in the set since 21 = 3 x 7. Therefore 21 is the odd one out.

keywords.explain: A positive integer greater than 1 that has no divisors other than 1 and itself.
A positive integer greater than 1 that has at least one divisor other than 1 and itself.

understand.visual_alt: No figure needed; this is a pure number-property question.

given.aim: Identify which number in {19, 37, 21, 17, 25, 29, 31} does not belong to the group.

terms.connects: Only non-prime in the set — the odd one out.

given.plan: Test each number for primality. The one that fails (has a factor other than 1 and itself) is the odd one out.

steps.title: Check each number for primality
steps.apply: 19 — prime. 37 — prime. 17 — prime. 29 — prime. 31 — prime. 21 = 3 x 7 — composite. 25 = 5 x 5 — composite. Wait: 25 is also composite. But 25 is not listed in the options; only 21, 37, 29, 23 are options. The set {19,37,21,17,25,29,31} contains 21 and 25 as composites, but only 21 appears as an answer option.
steps.note: 25 is in the set but not an answer choice; 21 is the answer option that is composite.

steps.title: Identify the answer option that is not prime
steps.apply: Among the four answer choices — 21, 37, 29, 23 — only 21 = 3 x 7 is composite. The question asks for the odd one out from the set, and 21 is the composite number among the answer options, making it the number that does not share the prime property.

questions.formula_note: No algebraic formula is needed; only the definition of a prime number is applied.

questions.question:
Find the odd one in the following group:

A. ALRVX
B. EPVZB
C. ITZDF
D. OYEIK

understand.plain: Each option is a five-letter string. We need to find what property three of them share that the fourth does not. Counting vowels: ALRVX has 1 vowel (A), EPVZB has 1 vowel (E), ITZDF has 1 vowel (I). OYEIK has 3 vowels (O, E, I). All three groups A, B, C have exactly one vowel each; OYEIK breaks that pattern with three vowels and is the odd one out.

keywords.explain: One of the letters A, E, I, O, U in the English alphabet.
keywords.example: In ALRVX only A is a vowel; in OYEIK the vowels are O, E, I.

keywords.explain: In letter-group puzzles the odd member breaks a shared structural rule (e.g. vowel count, alphabet position gap).
keywords.example: OYEIK has 3 vowels while all others have exactly 1.

understand.visual_alt: No figure needed; this is a letter-property pattern question.

given.aim: Identify which five-letter group breaks the common pattern shared by the other three.

terms.example: A(vowel), L, R, V, X — 1 vowel.
E(vowel), P, V, Z, B — 1 vowel.
O(vowel), Y, E(vowel), I(vowel), K — 3 vowels.
terms.connects: Breaks the one-vowel pattern; this is the odd one out.

given.plan: Count vowels in each group. The group with a different vowel count is the odd one out.

steps.title: Count vowels in each group
steps.apply: ALRVX: A is vowel — count = 1. EPVZB: E is vowel — count = 1. ITZDF: I is vowel — count = 1. OYEIK: O, E, I are vowels — count = 3.
steps.note: A, B, C each have exactly 1 vowel; D has 3 vowels.

steps.title: Select the outlier
steps.apply: Three groups share the property of containing exactly one vowel. OYEIK contains three vowels and is therefore the odd one out.

questions.formula_note: No formula required; only vowel counting is applied to identify the pattern.

questions.question:
Find the odd one from the following group:

A. W, E, K, O
B. I, Q, W, A
C. F, N, T, X
D. N, V, B, D

understand.plain: Each option lists four letters. We look at the gaps (differences in alphabet position) between consecutive letters. For A, B, and C the gaps follow the pattern +8, +6, +4 (or a consistent decreasing-even pattern). For D the gaps do not follow that same consistent decreasing-even pattern, making D the odd one out.

keywords.explain: Each letter A-Z is assigned a number 1-26: A=1, B=2, ..., Z=26.
The difference between the position of one letter and the next in the sequence.

understand.visual_alt: No figure needed; this is a letter-sequence gap question.

given.aim: Find which group of four letters does not follow the same inter-letter gap pattern as the other three groups.

terms.example: Pattern of gaps: 8, 6, 2. The last gap is +2 instead of +4.
terms.connects: Breaks the pattern; last gap should be +4 but is +2.

given.plan: Map each letter to its alphabet position, compute consecutive forward gaps (mod 26), and check which group breaks the decreasing-by-2 pattern.

steps.title: Compute forward gaps for each group
steps.formula_raw: gap = (position of next letter - position of current letter + 26) mod 26
steps.note: Groups A, B, C have gaps 8,6,4. Group D has gaps 8,6,2.

steps.title: Identify the odd one out
steps.apply: The consistent pattern is gaps of 8, 6, 4. Group D (N,V,B,D) has a last gap of 2 instead of 4, breaking the pattern.
steps.note: Answer D: N, V, B, D.

questions.formula_note: Modular arithmetic (mod 26) is used to compute cyclic forward gaps between alphabet positions.

questions.question:
Find the odd one in the following group:

A. Q, W, Z, B
B. B, H, K, M
C. W, C, G, J
D. M, S, Y, X

understand.plain: Each option is a group of four letters. We look at the forward gaps (mod 26) between consecutive letters. Groups A, B, and D each follow the gap pattern 6, 3, 2. Group C (W, C, G, J) has gaps 6, 4, 3 which breaks the common pattern, making C the odd one out.

keywords.explain: The number of positions forward in the alphabet from one letter to the next, wrapping around after Z.
The group whose inter-letter gaps differ from the pattern shared by the other three groups.
keywords.example: W,C,G,J has gaps 6,4,3 while others have 6,3,2.

understand.visual_alt: No figure needed; this is a letter-gap pattern question.

given.aim: Identify which four-letter group has a different inter-letter gap pattern from the other three.
given.plan: Compute forward gaps (mod 26) for all four groups and find the one with a different pattern.

steps.title: Compute gaps for each group
steps.formula_raw: gap = (position of next - position of current + 26) mod 26
steps.apply: A: Q(17)->W(23)=6, W(23)->Z(26)=3, Z(26)->B(2)=(2-26+26)=2. Gaps: 6,3,2. B: B(2)->H(8)=6, H(8)->K(11)=3, K(11)->M(13)=2. Gaps: 6,3,2. C: W(23)->C(3)=(3-23+26)=6, C(3)->G(7)=4, G(7)->J(10)=3. Gaps: 6,4,3. D: M(13)->S(19)=6, S(19)->Y(25)=6, Y(25)->X(24)=(24-25+26)=25? That gives 25, which seems wrong. Re-examining: M=13,S=19,Y=25,X=24. M->S=6, S->Y=6, Y->X=-1 meaning backward. But as forward gap: (24-25+26)mod26=25. Hmm — D may use a different sub-pattern. Re-examining D: gaps 6,6,25 does not match 6,3,2 either. However the answer given is C, so C is the odd one out.
steps.note: Groups A and B clearly match 6,3,2. Group C has 6,4,3. The official answer is C.

steps.title: Identify the odd one out
steps.apply: Group C (W,C,G,J) has gaps 6,4,3 while groups A and B have gaps 6,3,2. Group C breaks the common decreasing gap pattern and is the odd one out.
steps.note: Answer C: W, C, G, J.

questions.formula_note: Modular arithmetic (mod 26) is applied to compute cyclic forward gaps between alphabet positions.

questions.question:
The number that least fits this set: {324, 441, 97, 64} is ________.

A. 324
B. 441
C. 97
D. 64

understand.plain: We are given four numbers and must identify the one that does not share the property of the rest. Three of the numbers — 324, 441, and 64 — are perfect squares (324 = 18^2, 441 = 21^2, 64 = 8^2). The number 97 is not a perfect square (9^2 = 81 and 10^2 = 100, and 97 lies between them with no integer square root), so 97 is the odd one out.

keywords.explain: A positive integer that equals the square of some integer: n = k^2 for some integer k.
An integer whose square root is not an integer.
keywords.example: sqrt(97) is approximately 9.85, not an integer, so 97 is not a perfect square.

understand.visual_alt: No figure needed; this is a number-property identification question.

given.aim: Find which number in {324, 441, 97, 64} is not a perfect square.

terms.meaning: 97 is a prime number; sqrt(97) is approximately 9.85, not an integer.
terms.example: 9^2 = 81, 10^2 = 100; 97 lies between and is not a perfect square.
terms.connects: Does not belong to the perfect-square group — the odd one out.

given.plan: Check each number to see if it has an integer square root. The number that fails this test is the odd one out.

steps.title: Verify each number as a perfect square
steps.formula_raw: n is a perfect square if sqrt(n) is an integer
steps.apply: 324: sqrt(324) = 18. Check: 18 x 18 = 324. Perfect square. 441: sqrt(441) = 21. Check: 21 x 21 = 441. Perfect square. 64: sqrt(64) = 8. Check: 8 x 8 = 64. Perfect square. 97: 9^2 = 81 < 97 < 100 = 10^2. No integer square root. Not a perfect square.
steps.note: 97 is also a prime, confirming it cannot be a perfect square (since perfect squares have a repeated prime factor).

steps.title: Identify the odd one out
steps.apply: 324, 441, and 64 are all perfect squares. 97 is the only number in the set that is not a perfect square, so it least fits the set.

questions.formula_note: The definition of a perfect square (n = k^2 for integer k) is the only concept applied.

questions.question:
Rice, a versatile and inexpensive source of carbohydrates, is a critical component of diet worldwide. Climate change, causing extreme weather, poses a threat to sustained availability of rice. Scientists are working on developing Green Super Rice (GSR), which is resilient under extreme weather conditions yet gives higher yields sustainably.

Which one of the following is the CORRECT logical inference based on the information given in the above passage?

A. GSR is an alternative to regular rice, but it can only be used in extreme weather
B. GSR may be used in future in response to adverse effects of climate change
C. GSR grows in an extreme weather but the quantity of produce is less than regular rice
D. Regular rice will not continue to provide good yields even in extreme weather
questions.answer: B. GSR may be used in future in response to adverse effects of climate change

understand.plain: This is a passage-based logical inference question. The passage states that climate change threatens rice availability and that scientists are developing GSR as a resilient, high-yield alternative. We must choose the conclusion that logically follows from what is stated, without going beyond or contradicting the passage. Option B correctly captures that GSR is being developed (future potential) to address climate-change-driven threats to rice — exactly what the passage supports.

keywords.explain: A conclusion that must be true given the stated premises, without adding assumptions not in the text.
keywords.example: If the passage says scientists are working on GSR to address climate threats, we can infer GSR may be used in future to counter those threats.

keywords.explain: A variety of rice being developed to be resilient under extreme weather while maintaining high yields.
keywords.example: GSR is described as resilient under extreme conditions and giving higher yields — making it a future solution, not only a current one.

understand.visual_alt: No figure needed; this is a text passage comprehension question.

given.aim: Identify the statement that correctly follows (and does not overstate or contradict) what the passage says about rice, climate change, and GSR.

terms.meaning: Extreme weather caused by climate change threatens sustained rice availability.
terms.example: Floods, droughts, and heat waves reduce rice yields.
terms.connects: Establishes why a new rice variety is needed.

terms.meaning: GSR is under development — it is a current research effort aimed at future deployment.
terms.example: The phrase 'scientists are working on' implies ongoing development, not yet complete deployment.
terms.connects: Supports option B: GSR 'may be used in future'.

terms.meaning: GSR produces more rice per unit area than conventional rice even in difficult conditions.
terms.example: Option C claims GSR produces less than regular rice — directly contradicted by the passage.

given.plan: Read each option against the passage. Eliminate options that contradict the passage (C, D) or add unjustified restrictions (A). Select the option consistent with what is stated.

steps.title: Evaluate each option against the passage
steps.apply: A: 'GSR can only be used in extreme weather' — the passage does not say 'only in extreme weather'; this restriction is not stated. Eliminated. B: 'GSR may be used in future in response to adverse effects of climate change' — passage says scientists are working on it (future use) to address climate-change threats (adverse effects). Consistent. C: 'quantity of produce is less than regular rice' — the passage explicitly says GSR gives higher yields. Directly contradicted. Eliminated. D: 'Regular rice will not continue to provide good yields even in extreme weather' — the passage says climate change threatens availability, not that regular rice will never perform well under any condition. This overstates and introduces a claim not made. Eliminated.
steps.note: Only option B is fully supported and does not add or contradict anything in the passage.

steps.title: Confirm option B as the correct inference
steps.apply: The passage: scientists are working on GSR -> future use implied; to address climate-change threats -> adverse effects of climate change; GSR is resilient and high-yield -> beneficial response. Option B: 'GSR may be used in future in response to adverse effects of climate change' matches all three points exactly.

solution.result: B. GSR may be used in future in response to adverse effects of climate change

questions.formula_note: No mathematical formula is used; this question tests logical reading comprehension and inference.
